service_name: Information Security Strategy and Planning Services
description: Evaluates whether the organization has a defined information security program with sponsorship, vision, team, roadmap, and measurable strategy-and-planning outcomes.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: The information security strategy and planning function (or equivalent service) is established, funded, and operating effectively.

capabilities.name: Information Security Program
capabilities.description: A formal information security program exists with defined scope, objectives, and organizational coverage.
evaluation_focus: Program scope across business units and technology

capabilities.name: Security Vision and Mission
capabilities.description: Documented security vision and mission statements guide program direction and stakeholder expectations.
evaluation_focus: Communication and alignment with enterprise strategy

capabilities.name: Security Sponsor
capabilities.description: An executive security sponsor provides authority, advocacy, and accountability for the information security program.
evaluation_focus: Named executive sponsor for the program
Sponsor role in decisions, funding, and escalations
Regular sponsor engagement with security leadership

capabilities.name: Strategy and Planning Team
capabilities.description: A dedicated strategy and planning team (or clearly assigned roles) exists to develop and maintain security strategy and plans.
evaluation_focus: Team or role assignment for strategy and planning
Skills and capacity for planning activities
Collaboration with security operations and business stakeholders

capabilities.name: Security strategy alignment with business objectives
capabilities.description: Information security strategy is explicitly linked to business goals, risk appetite, and enterprise planning cycles.
evaluation_focus: Linkage to business objectives and risk appetite
Refresh cadence tied to business planning

capabilities.name: Information security program roadmap
capabilities.description: A security adoption roadmap for the information security program spans near-, mid-, and long-term horizons (including 3- and 5-year views where applicable).
evaluation_focus: Roadmap for the information security program
Initiative prioritization, dependencies, and change control

capabilities.name: Governance and accountability model
capabilities.description: Roles, committees, and decision rights for security strategy are defined and operating.

capabilities.name: Enterprise risk management integration
capabilities.description: Cyber and information security risks are integrated into enterprise risk registers and treatment planning.

capabilities.name: Policy and standards framework
capabilities.description: Policies and standards translate strategy into enforceable requirements across the organization.

capabilities.name: Budget and resource planning
capabilities.description: Security funding, staffing, and capability investments are planned and tracked against strategy.

capabilities.name: KPIs and KRIs
capabilities.description: Key performance and risk indicators measure security program health and inform leadership decisions.

capabilities.name: Regulatory and compliance alignment
capabilities.description: Strategy and planning account for applicable regulations, contracts, and assurance obligations.

capabilities.name: Third-party and supply chain security strategy
capabilities.description: Third-party risk is addressed in strategy with consistent requirements and oversight.

capabilities.name: Workforce and security culture planning
capabilities.description: Awareness, training, and culture initiatives are planned to support strategic outcomes.
